# Remote Sync Adapters — Zone Context

<zone>

Zone: Remote Sync Adapters (`remote-sync-adapters`)
Files: 18, Cohesion: 0.39, Coupling: 0.61
Risk: critical (score: 0.61)
Description: Manages remote synchronization with third-party project trackers (Jira, Notion) through a registry-based adapter pattern, including integration schemas and canonical ID mapping.

<findings>

[observation] [warning] 9 entry points — wide API surface, consider consolidating exports
[observation] [warning] High coupling (0.61) — 22 imports target "prd-schema-foundation"
[observation] [warning] Low cohesion (0.39) — files are loosely related, consider splitting this zone
[observation] [warning] Coupling of 0.61 slightly exceeds the 0.6 threshold; the sync CLI command pulls in adapter registry, integration schemas, and canonical mapping simultaneously — consider separating the CLI shim from the adapter infrastructure to reduce fan-out.
[observation] [info] Integration schemas for Jira and Notion are co-located under 'store/integration-schemas/' with a barrel index, which is correct; verify that the barrel does not re-export internal implementation details that should remain private to each adapter.
[observation] [info] The registry-based adapter architecture (adapter-registry.ts + per-vendor schema files) is a well-structured extension point that correctly isolates vendor-specific logic from the sync engine — a positive architectural pattern.
[suggestion] [info] Zone "remote-sync-adapters" has files across 8 directories — consider consolidating under a dedicated directory
[suggestion] [warning] Move Notion integration files into src/store/notion/ subdirectory (notion-client.ts → src/store/notion/client.ts, etc.). Without a subdirectory boundary, zone membership for Notion files is invisible to filesystem tools and determined solely by import graph proximity — a future Notion file added to src/store/ root will be silently absorbed into whichever zone the algorithm assigns.
[suggestion] [warning] src/store/index.ts and src/store/integration-schemas/index.ts expose two non-hierarchical barrel APIs for the same zone. Consumers can import from either without awareness of the other, making the store's complete public surface unknowable from any single entry point. Designate one as the authoritative public barrel and have the other re-export from it, or consolidate both into src/store/public.ts.
[suggestion] [warning] Zone "Remote Sync Adapters" (remote-sync-adapters) has critical risk (score: 0.61, cohesion: 0.39, coupling: 0.61) — requires refactoring before new feature development
[pattern] [warning] 9 entry points for an 18-file zone is a 50% entry-point ratio; a healthy module should expose a small fraction of its files as public API. Consolidating exports through a single src/store/public.ts would narrow the contract surface and enable safe internal restructuring.
[relationship] [warning] Three upstream zones import the store layer directly without a shared gateway, creating three independent implicit contracts. Per the CLAUDE.md gateway pattern a single store gateway is required; changes to adapter internals currently require simultaneous validation across all three consumers.
[anti-pattern] [warning] Notion integration files (notion-client.ts, notion-adapter.ts, notion-map.ts) coexist in src/store/ alongside generic adapter infrastructure (adapter-registry.ts, integration-schema.ts). Both the notion-sync-client zone and remote-sync-adapters zone draw from the same directory, meaning zone boundaries cannot be enforced or visualized through directory structure — the only boundary is the import graph, which is invisible to filesystem-based tools.
[anti-pattern] [warning] Two barrel index files (src/store/index.ts and src/store/integration-schemas/index.ts) coexist in the remote-sync-adapters zone without a declared hierarchy. Consumers importing from either barrel bypass the other, creating two implicit partial contracts for the same store layer and making it impossible to enforce a single public surface through code review alone.

</findings>

<insights>

- Low cohesion (0.39) — files are loosely related, consider splitting this zone
- High coupling (0.61) — 22 imports target "prd-schema-foundation"
- 9 entry points — wide API surface, consider consolidating exports
- The adapter-registry pattern with per-vendor integration schemas (jira.ts, notion.ts) is a clean extension point — adding a new integration requires only a new schema file and registry entry.
- Cohesion of 0.39 and coupling of 0.61 are both near warning thresholds; the sync CLI command being co-located with adapter infrastructure mixes transport concerns with domain adapters.
- The canonical.ts utility — responsible for ID mapping between local and remote items — is architecturally critical; ensure it has dedicated unit tests and is not modified without PRD-level review.
- Coupling of 0.61 slightly exceeds the 0.6 threshold; the sync CLI command pulls in adapter registry, integration schemas, and canonical mapping simultaneously — consider separating the CLI shim from the adapter infrastructure to reduce fan-out.
- The registry-based adapter architecture (adapter-registry.ts + per-vendor schema files) is a well-structured extension point that correctly isolates vendor-specific logic from the sync engine — a positive architectural pattern.
- Integration schemas for Jira and Notion are co-located under 'store/integration-schemas/' with a barrel index, which is correct; verify that the barrel does not re-export internal implementation details that should remain private to each adapter.
- Zone "remote-sync-adapters" has files across 8 directories — consider consolidating under a dedicated directory
- 9 entry points across 18 files yields a 50% entry-point ratio — far above a healthy module boundary. Most adapter internals (individual integration schemas, the registry, the store barrel) appear directly importable rather than mediated through a single public interface.
- The zone is consumed via three independent, unmediated import paths from three separate upstream zones (prd-cli-operations: 11, rex-mcp-service-layer: 9, prd-analysis-engine: 8) — in the absence of a shared gateway, each consumer has silently negotiated its own implicit contract with the store layer.
- Three upstream zones import the store layer directly without a shared gateway, creating three independent implicit contracts. Per the CLAUDE.md gateway pattern a single store gateway is required; changes to adapter internals currently require simultaneous validation across all three consumers.
- 9 entry points for an 18-file zone is a 50% entry-point ratio; a healthy module should expose a small fraction of its files as public API. Consolidating exports through a single src/store/public.ts would narrow the contract surface and enable safe internal restructuring.
- src/store/index.ts and src/store/integration-schemas/index.ts are two independent barrel exports within the same zone for the same store subdomain — neither declares itself as authoritative over the other, so consumers importing from either barrel negotiate their own implicit contract with the store layer.
- The Notion integration implementation (notion-client.ts, notion-adapter.ts, notion-map.ts) coexists in src/store/ alongside the generic adapter-registry.ts and integration-schema.ts files. Because both the notion-sync-client zone and the remote-sync-adapters zone draw from the same directory, file-level zone membership is determined by import graph proximity rather than directory structure — a future file move cannot cleanly separate the zones without also restructuring directory layout.
- Two barrel index files (src/store/index.ts and src/store/integration-schemas/index.ts) coexist in the remote-sync-adapters zone without a declared hierarchy. Consumers importing from either barrel bypass the other, creating two implicit partial contracts for the same store layer and making it impossible to enforce a single public surface through code review alone.
- Notion integration files (notion-client.ts, notion-adapter.ts, notion-map.ts) coexist in src/store/ alongside generic adapter infrastructure (adapter-registry.ts, integration-schema.ts). Both the notion-sync-client zone and remote-sync-adapters zone draw from the same directory, meaning zone boundaries cannot be enforced or visualized through directory structure — the only boundary is the import graph, which is invisible to filesystem-based tools.
- The Notion sub-domain files (notion-client.ts, notion-adapter.ts, notion-map.ts) have no naming prefix or directory grouping that distinguishes them from generic adapter infrastructure files (adapter-registry.ts, integration-schema.ts) within src/store/. This means a code reviewer cannot determine zone membership from filenames alone — the only signal is the import graph, which requires tooling to inspect.
- The zone's 9 entry points include both src/store/index.ts and src/store/integration-schemas/index.ts, but neither barrel re-exports the other. A consumer that imports from src/store/index.ts and another that imports from src/store/integration-schemas/index.ts are negotiating with different subsets of the same zone's API, creating an invisible API surface split within a single zone.
- Move Notion integration files into src/store/notion/ subdirectory (notion-client.ts → src/store/notion/client.ts, etc.). Without a subdirectory boundary, zone membership for Notion files is invisible to filesystem tools and determined solely by import graph proximity — a future Notion file added to src/store/ root will be silently absorbed into whichever zone the algorithm assigns.
- src/store/index.ts and src/store/integration-schemas/index.ts expose two non-hierarchical barrel APIs for the same zone. Consumers can import from either without awareness of the other, making the store's complete public surface unknowable from any single entry point. Designate one as the authoritative public barrel and have the other re-export from it, or consolidate both into src/store/public.ts.
- [call graph] 367 internal calls, 34 outgoing, 66 incoming (cohesion: 0.92, coupling: 0.08)

</insights>
